---
layout: paper
title: "Neuroformation™ v1.0 — Master Bibliography"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.19197818"
---

# Neuroformation™ v1.0 — Master Bibliography

**Back to:** [Main Paper](index.html) | [Visualizations](neuroformation-visualizations.html) | [Cross-References](neuroformation-cross-references.html)

*References contextualize observed patterns across neuroscience, performance science, and AI systems research. The Neuroformation™ architecture derives from practitioner observation and cross-domain application, not any single theoretical framework.*

---

## Cybernetics and Systems Theory

Altman, D. G., & Bland, J. M. (1995). Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. *BMJ*, 311(7003), 485. https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.311.7003.485

Beer, S. (1972). *Brain of the firm: The managerial cybernetics of organization*. Allen Lane.

Calabrese, E. J. (2008). Hormesis: Why it is important to toxicology and toxicologists. *Environmental Toxicology and Chemistry*, 27(7), 1451–1474. https://doi.org/10.1897/07-541.1

Friston, K. J. (2010). The free-energy principle: A unified brain theory? *Nature Reviews Neuroscience*, 11(2), 127–138. https://doi.org/10.1038/nrn2787

Hasson, U., Nastase, S. A., & Goldstein, A. (2020). Direct fit to nature: An evolutionary perspective on biological and artificial neural networks. *Neuron*, 105(3), 416–434. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuron.2019.12.002

Holland, J. H. (1992). *Adaptation in natural and artificial systems*. MIT Press.

Kauffman, S. A. (1993). *The origins of order: Self-organization and selection in evolution*. Oxford University Press.

McChrystal, S. (2015). *Team of teams: New rules of engagement for a complex world*. Portfolio/Penguin.

Perrow, C. (1984). *Normal accidents: Living with high-risk technologies*. Basic Books.

Reason, J. (1997). *Managing the risks of organizational accidents*. Ashgate.

Snowden, D. J., & Boone, M. E. (2007). A leader's framework for decision making. *Harvard Business Review*, 85(11), 68–76.

Taleb, N. N. (2012). *Antifragile: Things that gain from disorder*. Random House.

von Bertalanffy, L. (1968). *General system theory*. George Braziller.

Wiener, N. (1948). *Cybernetics: Or control and communication in the animal and the machine*. John Wiley & Sons.

---

## Computational Substrate Theory

Chalmers, D. J. (1996). *The conscious mind: In search of a fundamental theory*. Oxford University Press.

Dennett, D. C. (1991). *Consciousness explained*. Little, Brown.

Putnam, H. (1967). Psychological predicates. In W. H. Capitan & D. D. Merrill (Eds.), *Art, mind, and religion*. University of Pittsburgh Press.

Tegmark, M. (2014). *Our mathematical universe*. Knopf.

---

## Narrative Identity Architecture

Bruner, J. (1986). *Actual minds, possible worlds*. Harvard University Press.

Herman, J. L. (1992). *Trauma and recovery*. Basic Books.

McAdams, D. P. (1993). *The stories we live by: Personal myths and the making of the self*. William Morrow.

Ricoeur, P. (1992). *Oneself as another*. University of Chicago Press.

---

## Applied Performance Science and Motor Learning

Agresti, A., & Franklin, C. (2013). *Statistics: The art and science of learning from data* (3rd ed.). Pearson.

Akerlof, G. A., & Kranton, R. E. (2000). Economics and identity. *Quarterly Journal of Economics*, 115(3), 715–753. https://doi.org/10.1162/003355300554881

Arnsten, A. F. T. (2009). Stress signalling pathways that impair prefrontal cortex structure and function. *Nature Reviews Neuroscience*, 10(6), 410–422. https://doi.org/10.1038/nrn2648

Beilock, S. L., & Carr, T. H. (2001). On the fragility of skilled performance: What governs choking under pressure? *Journal of Experimental Psychology: General*, 130(4), 701–725. https://doi.org/10.1037/0096-3445.130.4.701

Berardi, J. M., & Andrews, R. (2019). *The essentials of sport and exercise nutrition* (3rd ed.). Precision Nutrition.

Brown, B. (2010). *The gifts of imperfection*. Hazelden.

Ericsson, K. A., Krampe, R. T., & Tesch-Römer, C. (1993). The role of deliberate practice in the acquisition of expert performance. *Psychological Review*, 100(3), 363–406. https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-295X.100.3.363

Eysenck, M. W., Derakshan, N., Santos, R., & Calvo, M. G. (2007). Anxiety and cognitive performance: Attentional control theory. *Emotion*, 7(2), 336–353. https://doi.org/10.1037/1528-3542.7.2.336

Fitts, P. M., & Posner, M. I. (1967). *Human performance*. Brooks/Cole.

Grossman, P., Ackland, G. L., Allen, A. M., Berntson, G. G., Booth, L. C., Burghardt, G. M., et al. (2026). Challenging claims in the field of heart rate variability. *Clinical Neuropsychiatry*, 23(1), 100–112. https://doi.org/10.36131/cnfioritieditore20260110

Gütlin, D., & Auksztulewicz, R. (2025). Predictive coding algorithms induce brain-like responses in artificial neural networks. *PLOS Complex Systems*, 2(11), e0000076. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcsy.0000076

Lally, P., van Jaarsveld, C. H. M., Potts, H. W. W., & Wardle, J. (2010). How are habits formed: Modelling habit formation in the real world. *European Journal of Social Psychology*, 40(6), 998–1009. https://doi.org/10.1002/ejsp.674

LeDoux, J. E. (1996). *The emotional brain*. Simon & Schuster.

Markus, H. (1977). Self-schemata and processing information about the self. *Journal of Personality and Social Psychology*, 35(2), 63–78. https://doi.org/10.1037/0022-3514.35.2.63

McKenzie, I. A., et al. (2014). Motor skill learning requires active central myelination. *Science*, 346(6207), 318–322. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.1254960

Nørretranders, T. (1998). *The user illusion: Cutting consciousness down to size*. Viking.

Pfaff, D., & McMillan, S. (2019). *The ALTIS performance therapy course*. ALTIS.

Schmidt, R. A. (1975). A schema theory of discrete motor skill learning. *Psychological Review*, 82(4), 225–260. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0076770

Thayer, J. F., Hansen, A. L., Saus-Rose, E., & Johnsen, B. H. (2009). Heart rate variability, prefrontal neural function, and cognitive performance. *Annals of Behavioral Medicine*, 37(2), 141–153. https://doi.org/10.1007/s12160-009-9101-z

Vealey, R. S. (2007). Mental skills training in sport. In G. Tenenbaum & R. C. Eklund (Eds.), *Handbook of sport psychology* (3rd ed., pp. 285–309). Wiley.

Wulf, G., McNevin, N., & Shea, C. H. (2001). The automaticity of complex motor skill learning as a function of attentional focus. *Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*, 54A(4), 1143–1154. https://doi.org/10.1080/713756012

Xiao, L., et al. (2016). Rapid production of new oligodendrocytes is required in the earliest stages of motor-skill learning. *Nature Neuroscience*, 19(9), 1210–1217. https://doi.org/10.1038/nn.4351

Zheng, J., & Meister, M. (2025). Revealing the computational structure of the human visual cortex with a deep neural network. *Neuron*, 113(2), 192–204. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuron.2024.11.008

---

## Cognitive Neuroscience

Gallagher, S. (2017). *Enactivist interventions: Rethinking the mind*. Oxford University Press.

Gallagher, S. (2023). *Embodied and enactive approaches to cognition*. Cambridge University Press.

Hofstadter, D. R. (1979). *Gödel, Escher, Bach: An eternal golden braid*. Basic Books.

Hollnagel, E., Woods, D. D., & Leveson, N. (Eds.). (2006). *Resilience engineering: Concepts and precepts*. Ashgate.

Kahneman, D. (2011). *Thinking, fast and slow*. Farrar, Straus and Giroux.

Varela, F. J., Thompson, E., & Rosch, E. (1991). *The embodied mind*. MIT Press.

---

## APP Published Papers

Slusher, A. M. (2026a). Elevation Grid™ v1.1: A field-developed framework for high-stakes performance across adaptive and elite populations. *Achieve Peak Performance*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18790842

Slusher, A. M. (2026b). Neural Access Method™: Transmission protocol for high-pressure intervention. *Achieve Peak Performance*. Manuscript in preparation.

Slusher, A. M. (2026c). Memory Breathing Methodology (MBM) v1.0. *ValorGrid Solutions*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18790096

## VGS Published Papers

Slusher, A. M. (2025a). Cognitive Mage v1.0. *ValorGrid Solutions Technical Reports*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17643267

Slusher, A. M. (2025b). Complete Symbolic Fracture Cascade v1.0. *ValorGrid Solutions Technical Reports*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17309239

Slusher, A. M. (2025c). Distributed Cognitive Network v1.0. *ValorGrid Solutions Technical Reports*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17555568

Slusher, A. M. (2025d). Mythopoeic Intelligence Agents v1.0. *ValorGrid Solutions Technical Reports*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17770533

Slusher, A. M. (2025e). Synoetic OS v1.0. *ValorGrid Solutions Technical Reports*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17808864

Slusher, A. M. (2026d). PME v1.0: Predictive Myelination Engine. *ValorGrid Solutions Technical Reports*. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18318485

---

*2026 © ValorGrid Solutions. [Main Paper](index.html)*
